AIResultAILevel.description:
AI severity level.

 - AI_LEVEL_CRITICAL: Critical AI SAST.
 - AI_LEVEL_HIGH: High AI SAST.
 - AI_LEVEL_MEDIUM: Medium AI SAST.
 - AI_LEVEL_LOW: Low AI SAST.

dataflow.description:
Deprecated: use call_stack instead.
One or more source locations representing the data flow path from a source to a sink.
The locations are ordered and include the source, any intermediate nodes, and the sink.
If only a single location is provided, it represents the sink location only.

language.description: Programming language of the file where this result was found.

location.description: The main location of the vulnerability.

ContextContextType.description:
- CONTEXT_TYPE_MAIN: Objects from a scan of the default branch.
All objects in the oss namespace are in the main context.
The context id is always "default".
 - CONTEXT_TYPE_EXTERNAL: Indicates that this object is a copy/temporary value of an object in
another project. Used for same-tenant dependencies. In source code
reference this is equivalent to "vendor" folders. Package versions in
the external context are only scanned for call graphs. No other
operations are performed on them.
 - CONTEXT_TYPE_CI_RUN: Objects from a PR scan. The context id is the PR UUID.
Objects in this context are deleted after 30 days.
 - CONTEXT_TYPE_SBOM: Objects from an SBOM scan. The context id is the SBOM serial number or
some other unique identifier.
 - CONTEXT_TYPE_REF: Objects from a scan of a specific branch. The context id is the branch
reference name.

GroupResponseGroupData.description: Information about objects matching the given key.

aggregation_count.description:
Number of objects in group.
This field is always set.

aggregation_uuids.description:
List of UUIDs of the objects in the group.
Only populated if show_aggregation_uuids is set.

aggregation_value.description: Value of the aggregation operation as calculated by the query.

unique_counts.description:
Map of counts for the given unique_count_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_count_paths is set.

unique_values.description:
Map of values for the given unique_value_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_value_paths is set.

LinterCorrectnessAnalysisCodeFix.description: CodeFix represents a potential fix to a determined true positive linter result.

file_path.description: The file path of the code fix.

line_end.description: The end line number of the code fix.

line_start.description: The start line number of the code fix.

patch.description: The code fix to be applied.

LinterCorrectnessAnalysisDataFlowNode.description: DataFlowNode represents a node in the data flow trace from source to sink.

code_line_num.description: Line number in the source file.

code_snippet.description: Variable or expression at this point in the flow.

type.description: Type of node in the data flow (source, step, or sink).

properties.description: Key-value pairs that provide additional information.

SarifSuppressionKind.description:
- KIND_IN_SOURCE: Suppression is in the source code.
 - KIND_EXTERNAL: Suppression is external to the source code.

SarifThreadFlowLocationImportance.description: Specifies the importance of this location in understanding the code flow.

SpecEndorFingerprint.description:
An engine independent and rule agnostic fingerprint computed based on
source location information.

SpecLinterResultLevel.description:
The severity level of the linter result. Crafted using the Semgrep specification.

 - LINTER_RESULT_LEVEL_ERROR: ERROR indicates that a serious problem was found.
 - LINTER_RESULT_LEVEL_WARNING: WARNING indicates that a problem that is not considered serious was found.
 - LINTER_RESULT_LEVEL_INFO: INFO indicates that the notification is purely informational.
 - LINTER_RESULT_LEVEL_DEBUG: DEBUG indicates that this is a trace notification (debug).

SpecLinterResultOrigin.description:
The origin of the linter result.

 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_SEMGREP: The semgrep tool reported this result.
 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_SECRETS_SCANNER: The secrets scanner reported this result.
 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_SAST_SCANNER: The SAST scanner reported this result.
 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_LLM_SCANNER: The LLM scanner reported this result.
 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_AI_SAST_SCANNER: The AI SAST scanner reported this result.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

v1AIMetaConfidenceLevel.description:
Confidence level for the finding.

 - CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_CRITICAL: Critical finding.
 - CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_HIGH: Very important findings.
 - CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_MEDIUM: Important findings.
 - CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_LOW: Low priority finding.

level.description: The severity level of the AI SAST.

call_stack.description: The path (call stack) of the vulnerable code.

sanitizer_callsites.description: This are the locations of the callsites that are used to mitigate the vulnerability.

sanitizer_definitions.description: The sanitizer definitions locations that are used to mitigate the vulnerability.

v1Context.description: Contexts keep objects from different scans separated.

id.description: The context ID, such as a pull request ID or branch reference.

tags.description:
A list of tags applied to a context. Used primarily for CI and SBOM
contexts.

type.description: Context type, usually defined based on how endorctl is being used.

will_be_deleted_at.description:
Time that all objects in this context will be deleted.
This field is deprecated and will be removed in the future.
Please use the meta.will_be_deleted_at field instead.

v1Correctness.description:
Correctness represents a correctness assessment.

 - CORRECTNESS_TRUE_POSITIVE: The AI analysis result is a true positive.
 - CORRECTNESS_FALSE_POSITIVE: The AI analysis result is a false positive.
 - CORRECTNESS_FALSE_NEGATIVE: The AI analysis result is a false negative.
 - CORRECTNESS_UNKNOWN: The AI analysis result is unknown.

v1CorrectnessAnalyzer.description: CorrectnessAnalyzer represents the analyzer used to determine the correctness of a linter result.

v1CountResponse.description: Response to a list count request.

count.description: Number of objects matching the given list parameters.

v1DistributionFormatType.description:
Distribution format type.

 - DISTRIBUTION_FORMAT_TYPE_PYTHON_SOURCE: Python source was used to build this package.
 - DISTRIBUTION_FORMAT_TYPE_PYTHON_EGG: Egg-info based distribution format for python.
 - DISTRIBUTION_FORMAT_TYPE_PYTHON_WHEEL: Dist-info based distribution format for python.

v1Ecosystem.description:
- ECOSYSTEM_GO: GoLang.
 - ECOSYSTEM_MAVEN: Maven.
 - ECOSYSTEM_PYPI: Python.
 - ECOSYSTEM_CARGO: Rust.
 - ECOSYSTEM_NPM: Javascript.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GEM: Ruby.
 - ECOSYSTEM_NUGET: Dotnet.
 - ECOSYSTEM_PACKAGIST: PHP.
 - ECOSYSTEM_SBOM: SBOMs.
 - ECOSYSTEM_RPM: RPM.
 - ECOSYSTEM_DEBIAN: Debian.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GITHUB_ACTION: GitHub Actions.
 - ECOSYSTEM_COCOAPOD: Cocoapods.
 - ECOSYSTEM_APK: APK (alpine et.al).
 - ECOSYSTEM_CONTAINER: Containers.
 - ECOSYSTEM_HUGGING_FACE: Hugging Face.
 - ECOSYSTEM_C: C/C++.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GIT: ecosystem GIT for GIT repository dependencies.
This can be used for package name of the resolved dependencies when a
given repository has dependencies to other GIT repositories. Currently we
use this to represent vulnerabilities for the given GIT repository. ex:
git submodules, C/C++ dependencies.
 - ECOSYSTEM_AI_MODEL: AI models.
 - ECOSYSTEM_SWIFT: Ecosystem Swift consists of native Swift packages, which are defined
using the Package.swift manifest file and managed by the Swift Package
Manager. There is a separate ecosystem for Cocoapod packages called
ECOSYSTEM_COCOAPOD, which is an alternative package manager for Swift
packages.

v1FindingClassification.description:
Classification of the finding type. Extensible for future categories.

 - FINDING_CLASSIFICATION_SECURITY_VULNERABILITY: Security vulnerability (e.g. SQL injection, XSS, hardcoded creds).
 - FINDING_CLASSIFICATION_LOGICAL_BUG: Logical bug (e.g. null pointer, resource leak, race condition).

v1GroupAggregationValueResponse.description:
GroupAggregationValueResponse returns the value of the aggregation if
requested.

v1GroupResponse.description: Response to a list group request.

v1IndexData.description:
IndexData is used to index the resource for search. It's an internal
object.

search_score.description: search_score is the score of the resource for search. Internal use only.

will_be_deleted_at.description: Time that the resource will be deleted.

v1LinterCorrectnessAnalysis.description: LinterCorrectnessAnalysis represents an analysis of a linter result.

analysis_summary.description: Analysis summary, providing an explanation of the linter result and classification.

analyzer.description: The analyzer of the linter correctness analysis.

code_fixes.description: Code fixes are potential fixes to the identified issue.

code_references.description: Textual description of line references/ fields/variables , etc for the linter result.

confidence_level.description: The confidence level of the linter correctness analysis.

correctness.description: The correctness assessment of the linter correctness analysis.

data_flow.description: Textual Description of the source to sink data flow analysis for the linter result.

description.description: The textual description of the linter correctness analysis.

risk_assessment.description: Risk assessment for the linter result. Provides False/True positive classifaction and reasoning for the linter result..

sanitizers.description: The sanitizers used to determine the correctness of the linter result.

security_control.description: Description of any sanitizers, validators, or mitigations found (or lack thereof) for the linter result.

security_impact.description: Description of the security impact/potential consequences for the linter result.

symbols.description: Key variables/symbols in the data flow path for the linter result.

technical_detail.description: Further technical details (eg results about the vulnerability type and exploitation potential) for the linter result.

version.description: The version of the linter correctness analysis.

v1LinterResult.description: The meta parent_kind can take one of two values - PackageVersion or RepositoryVersion.

context.description:
Context for the repository version will be automatically updated
to that of the version, unless it is a CI run.

tenant_meta.description: Linter results are associated with a tenant and namespace.

uuid.description: The UUID of a linter result.

distribution_format.description: The distribution format of the package.

extra_key.description:
Additional information that may result in creating a unique linter result.
In some cases we may receive multiple results for the same project and the same file and we
want to have multiple entries for the same field like parent_uuid, meta.name, and physical_location.
By setting different values in this field, we will be able to create a separate result.
Essentially, we will have a unique entry per field.

fingerprints.description:
The list and count of found fingerprints. The counter is maintained
to ease the retrieval of these records in the analytics phase.

linter_correctness_analyses.description: An analysis of the linter result.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project to which this result is related.

ref.description: The Git reference of the repository version.

storage_location.description: The storage location of the package related to this linter result.

suppressed.description: Result was suppressed by semgrep (e.g. because of a "nosemgrep" code annotation).

v1ListLinterResultsResponse.description: Response to the request to list linter results.

next_page_id.description: The UUID of last message retrieved.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token that can be used to retrieve the next page of results.

v1LocationType.description:
Classification of the location type within a data flow path.

 - LOCATION_TYPE_SOURCE: The source location.
 - LOCATION_TYPE_SINK: The sink location.
 - LOCATION_TYPE_INTERMEDIATE: The intermediate location.
 - LOCATION_TYPE_LOGICAL_BUG: The logical bug location.

v1LocationWithName.description: A code location with a name and type.

location.description: The location of the code in a file.

name.description:
The (preferably) unique name of the code location.
Could be a function ref or a module name.

type.description: The type of the code location.

v1Meta.description: Common fields for all Endor Labs resources.

annotations.description:
Annotations can be used to attach metadata to a resource message.
Annotation values can be small or large, structured or unstructured,
and may include characters not permitted by labels.
The keys may contain alphanumerics, underscores (_), dots (.) and dashes
(-). The values of an annotation must be 16384 bytes or smaller.

create_time.description:
Time the resource was created.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

created_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the user who created the object,
for example, ewok@endor.ai@google@api-key.

description.description: Resource description. Must be less than 1024 bytes.

kind.description:
Resource kind, for example, HelloResponse.
Auto-generated using the protobuf message proto.MessageName().Name().

name.description: Resource name. Must be 63 characters or less.

parent_kind.description: Parent object resource kind, for example, Project.

references.description: Map of objects referenced in a query API.

tags.description:
List of tags attached to the resource.
Tags can be used to select objects and to find collections of objects that
satisfy certain conditions. A tag must be 255 characters or less.

update_time.description:
Time the resource was last updated.
Note: Updated on all create/patch/delete operations.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

updated_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the last user who updated the object,
for example, vulnerabilityingestor@endor.ai@x509.

upsert_time.description:
Time the resource was last upserted.

Note:
create_time is only set the first time the resource is created.
upsert_time is set every time the resource is upseted.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

rendered.title: A rendered representation of the content

uri_base_id.title: Base URI identifier for resolving relative paths

v1SarifCodeFlow.description: Code flow object representing a sequence of code locations that specify a data flow path.

message.title: A message relevant to the code flow

thread_flows.title: An array of one or more unique threadFlow objects

commit_message.title: Commit message associated with the change

commit_sha.title: Git commit SHA where the finding was detected

hash.title: Hash of the primary location line for deduplication

id.title: Unique identifier based on matching criteria

annotations.title: An array of regions that describe regions within the artifact specified by the location object that is relevant to the location

id.description: A unique identifier for this location object.

logical_locations.description: The logical locations associated with a physical location.

message.title: A message relevant to the location

physical_location.title: Physical location information for the finding

relationships.description: An array of relationships between this location and other locations.

v1SarifLogicalLocation.description: A logical location that can be used to locate an artifact.

decorated_name.description: The name of the decorated logical location.

fully_qualified_name.description: The fully qualified name of the logical location.

index.description: The 0-based index of this logical location in an array of logical locations.

name.description: The name of the logical location.

parent_index.description: The name of the parent of this logical location.

properties.description: Key/value pairs that provide additional information.

end_column.title: Ending column number in the file

end_line.title: Ending line number in the file

start_column.title: Starting column number in the file

start_line.title: Starting line number in the file

kinds.description: A set of strings that describe the relationship.

properties.description: Key/value pairs that provide additional information about the relationship.

target.description: The id of the location that is the target of the relationship.

v1SarifReportingDescriptorReference.description: A reference to a reporting descriptor.

guid.description: The globally unique identifier for the referenced reporting descriptor.

id.description: The id of the referenced reporting descriptor.

index.description: The index of the referenced reporting descriptor in the toolComponent.rules array.

tool_component.description: A reference to the tool component that contains the referenced reporting descriptor.

code_flows.description: Code flow object representing a sequence of code locations that specify a data flow path.

fingerprints.title: Complete fingerprint of the finding, used for deduplication

locations.title: List of locations in the code where the finding was detected

partial_fingerprints.title: Partial fingerprint of the finding, used for approximate matching

rule_id.title: Unique identifier of the rule that triggered this result

action_policies_triggered.title: List of action policies that were triggered by this finding

categories.title: List of categories the finding belongs to

cvss_score.title: Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) score, ranging from 0.0 to 10.0

cvss_vector.title: CVSS vector string describing the characteristics of the vulnerability

cvss_version.title: The version of the CVSS score used

epss_percentile_score.title: EPSS percentile score, showing how severe the vulnerability is compared to others

epss_probability_score.title: Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) probability score, indicating likelihood of exploitation

explanation.title: Detailed explanation of the finding and its implications

finding_url.title: URL to view the finding in the Endor platform

impact_score.title: Custom impact score assigned to the finding based on business context

project_uuid.title: Unique identifier for the project where the finding was discovered

remediation.title: Recommended steps to fix or mitigate the finding

tags.title: List of tags associated with the finding, used for categorization and filtering

message.title: A message relevant to this call stack

location.title: The location of the stack frame

module.title: The name of the module that contains the location to which this stack frame refers

parameters.title: The parameters passed to the function

v1SarifThreadFlow.description: Thread flow object representing a sequence of code locations executed by a single thread.

id.title: A unique identifier for the thread flow within the code flow

immutable_state.title: Values of relevant expressions at the start of the thread flow that remain constant

initial_state.title: Values of relevant expressions at the start of the thread flow

locations.title: The locations that were executed during this thread flow

message.title: A message relevant to the thread flow

v1SarifThreadFlowLocation.description: A location visited by an analysis tool in the course of simulating or tracing the execution of a program.

execution_order.title: An integer representing the temporal order in which execution reached this location

execution_time_utc.title: The time at which execution reached this location

index.title: The index of the thread flow location within the thread flow

kinds.title: An array of strings that classify the location

module.title: The name of the module that contains the code location

nesting_level.title: An integer representing the level of nesting within the thread flow

stack.title: The call stack leading to this location

state.title: Values of relevant expressions at this location

taxa.description: A set of distinct strings that categorize the thread flow location.

web_request.title: Web request associated with the thread flow location

web_response.title: Web response associated with the thread flow location

v1SarifToolComponentReference.description: A reference to a tool component.

guid.description: The globally unique identifier for the tool component.

index.description: The index of the tool component in the tool.extensions array.

name.description: The name of the tool component.

index.title: The index of the request within run.webRequests
The index of the response within run.webResponses

no_response_received.title: Set to true if the response was intercepted

fs_scanned.title: Set when the secret was discovered by looking into the filesystem

git_log_scanned.description:
Set when the secret was discovered by looking in the Git logs using the
full history option.

secret_id.description:
Same value for two linter results with the same secret found in different
locations.

v1SemgrepTriggerPattern.description: A pattern that triggered the vulnerability scan.

end_column.title: Ending column number in the file

end_line.title: Ending line number in the file

function_name.description: The name of the function for this location if available.

relative_path.description: The relative path to the file where the issue was found.

start_column.title: Starting column number in the file

start_line.title: Starting line number in the file

v1TenantMeta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

namespace.description:
Namespaces are a way to organize organizational units into virtual
groupings of resources. Namespaces must be a fully qualified name,
for example, the child namespace of namespace "endor.prod" called "app"
is called "endor.prod.app".

v1ValidationStatus.description:
Status of secret validation.

 - VALIDATION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: No validator.
 - VALIDATION_STATUS_FAILURE: Failed to validate secret.
 - VALIDATION_STATUS_SECRET_IS_VALID: Secret is valid.
 - VALIDATION_STATUS_SECRET_IS_INVALID: Secret is invalid
